quotes.quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.
quotes.rationale: Identifies the LLM tournament as a ranking evaluator over drug candidates using supporting literature.

quotes.quote: When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.
quotes.rationale: Reports agreement and consistency evidence for the LLM judge's preferences against expert preferences.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
quotes.rationale: Shows Robin uses experimental data analysis to drive a new round of candidate generation.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.
quotes.rationale: Identifies researchers as the source of experimental execution and data provision.

quotes.quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
quotes.rationale: Shows a human performed part of sequencing data processing before Finch analysis.

quotes.quote: Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.
quotes.rationale: Identifies human analysis of flow cytometry data as an assay-analysis channel.

quotes.quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.
quotes.rationale: Reports that Robin's generated experimental outlines are not yet precise executable laboratory protocols.

diagnostics.consequence: Candidate ranking has reported agreement with experts, but the packet does not report a systematic error, bias, or probabilistic calibration study for the LLM judge.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.

diagnostics.statement: The LLM ranking channel reports agreement and intra-rater consistency evidence, but not bias testing or probabilistic calibration.

diagnostics.consequence: Human translation remains necessary before laboratory execution, limiting autonomous executability of proposed experiments.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.

diagnostics.statement: Robin does not yet produce precise, executable experimental protocols.

diagnostics.consequence: Claims about autonomous experimental-data interpretation are harder to separate from human preprocessing or human assay interpretation in the reported implementation.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

diagnostics.statement: The packet reports Robin autonomous analysis, while also reporting human preprocessing and human flow-cytometry analysis.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.

recommendations.proposed_action: Add validation of whether generated experimental protocols are directly executable with minimal human translation.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Protocols require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.
success_check.evidence_needed: A documented assessment of Robin-generated protocol outputs by laboratory executors.
success_check.observable: Fraction of generated protocols judged precise and executable.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

recommendations.proposed_action: Report the boundary between human preprocessing, human assay analysis, Finch analysis, and Robin interpretation in each experimental-data workflow.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Every reported assay-analysis step is assigned to human, Finch, Robin, or another actor.
success_check.evidence_needed: A workflow trace or methods table mapping each data-processing step to its executor and downstream use.
success_check.observable: Step-level attribution coverage for experimental data processing.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.

recommendations.proposed_action: Extend LLM-judge evaluation beyond expert-preference agreement to include systematic error or bias testing for candidate ranking.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Bias or systematic-error results are reported separately from agreement and intra-rater consistency.
success_check.evidence_needed: A property-specific error or bias study over the LLM-judged tournament outputs.
success_check.observable: Documented systematic error or bias metrics for candidate-ranking decisions.

memory_structure: experimental results provided to Robin for autonomous analysis
new round of therapeutic candidates generated from interpreted analysis results
policy_type: LLM-judged tournament ranking of drug candidates
